methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $380M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Harbinger
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
ZeroAvia is a Series C-stage transportation company founded by Valery Miftakhov. The company has raised $380M+ to date. The valuation universe comprises seven private peers in the transportation sector, including Einride, Harbinger, Rivian, The Boring Company, and REGENT Craft; no public comparables exist in this narrow frontier-tech transportation segment.

The stage-progression method is the applicable triangulation approach here. Using $380M in total capital raised as a proxy for baseline valuation and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C-to-next-stage advancement, the method implies a valuation of $646M. A peer-median approach was not feasible; while five same-sector peers exist, insufficient overlap occurred across same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier filters to meet the minimum threshold of three datapoints, and a broader all-sector median would lack precision given the specialized nature of frontier transportation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $549M to $743M, with a central estimate of $646M. This range reflects reasonable variance bounds around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the strength of ZeroAvia's technical pathway relative to the peer set, near-term funding and development milestones, and regulatory momentum in hydrogen-electric aviation—factors not quantifiable from available data. The single-method foundation should be regarded as foundational rather than definitive pending access to comparable transaction or financing round details.
